zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Harrington於1411年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾維斯特利時，她的父親Sir James Harington42歲，母親Ellen DeUrswick47歲。她和Thomas Lumley至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1476年在英國英格蘭達勒姆達勒姆享年65歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Harrington于1411年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾維斯特利时，她的父亲Sir James Harington42岁，母亲Ellen DeUrswick47岁。她和Thomas Lumley至少有3个儿子和3个女儿。她去世于1476年在英国英格兰達勒姆達勒姆享年65岁。

nameMeanings.name: Harrington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Harrington (Cumberland, Lincolnshire, Northamptonshire). The Cumberland placename derives from the Old English personal name Hæfer + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Lincolnshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Hearra + Old English connective -ing- + tūn. The Northamptonshire derives from an Old English personal name Hǣthhere + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Herendeen .
Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArrachtáin ‘descendant of Arrachtán’, a personal name from a diminutive of arrachtach ‘mighty, powerful’.
Irish: in Kerry, this name was adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hIongardail, later Ó hUrdáil, ‘descendant of Iongardal’, a personal name of uncertain origin.

variants.name: Herington
meanings: English: variant of   Harrington   or   Herrington  .

variants.name: Herrington
meanings: habitational name from Heronden in Tenterden (Kent) or Herrington in Houghton (Durham). The Kent placename may derive from an Old English personal name Hȳra + Old English connective -ing- + denn ‘pasture’, while the Durham placename may come from Old English hæring ‘rocky place’ (from hær ‘rock, heap of stones’ + the placename forming suffix -ing) or hæren ‘rocky’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Arrington
meanings: variant of Harrington with loss of initial H-.
possibly a habitational name from Arrington, a place in Cambridgeshire, named from an Old English byname, Earn(a), meaning ‘eagle’ + -inga- ‘people or followers of’ + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Harrington was born in 1411, in Westleigh, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir James Harington, was 42 and her mother, Ellen DeUrswick, was 47. She had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Thomas Lumley. She died in 1476, in Durham, Durham, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Harrington est née en 1411, à Westleigh, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir James Harington, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Ellen DeUrswick, avait 47 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Thomas Lumley. Elle est décédée en 1476, à Durham, Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Harrington 1411 in Westleigh, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir James Harington 42 und ihre Mutter Ellen DeUrswick 47. Sie hatte mit Thomas Lumley mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1476 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Durham, Durham, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Harrington è nata il nel 1411 nel Westleigh, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir James Harington, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Ellen DeUrswick, aveva 47 anni. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Thomas Lumley. Morì nel 1476, nel Durham, Durham, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 65 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Harrington(이)가 1411년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 웨스틀리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir James Harington(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ellen DeUrswick(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 Thomas Lumley(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1476년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 뒤람에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.
